status.en: the social or professional position of someone compared to others

ex.en: Her status in the company rose quickly.

status.en: the current state of something at a particular time

ex.en: What's the status of the project?

steadily.en: in a steady, continuous way, without sudden changes

ex.en: The company's profits grew steadily over the years.
ex.ko: 그 회사의 이익은 수년간 꾸준히 증가했다.

steady.en: not changing or moving; staying the same

ex.en: He has a steady job and a steady income.
ex.ko: 그는 안정적인 직장과 꾸준한 수입이 있다.

steady.en: happening or moving in a regular, continuous way

ex.en: There has been a steady increase in prices.

steady.en: to make something calm or stop it from shaking

ex.en: She steadied herself against the wall.

steam.en: the hot gas that water turns into when it boils

ex.en: Steam rose from the hot cup of tea.

steam.en: to cook food using the hot vapor from boiling water

ex.en: Steam the vegetables for five minutes.

steel.en: a strong hard metal made from iron, used for building and making tools

ex.en: The bridge is made of steel.

steel.en: to prepare yourself mentally to deal with something difficult

ex.en: She steeled herself for the bad news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나쁜 소식에 대비해 마음을 굳게 먹었다.

steep.en: rising or falling sharply; hard to climb

ex.en: They climbed a steep hill.

steep.en: (of a price or demand) unreasonably high

ex.en: $50 for a sandwich is a bit steep.

stem.en: the long thin part of a plant that supports leaves or flowers

ex.en: She cut the flower's stem too short.

stem.en: to come from or be caused by something

ex.en: Her fear of dogs stems from a childhood accident.
ex.ko: 그녀의 개에 대한 두려움은 어린 시절 사고에서 비롯되었다.

ex.en: The dog fetched the stick.

stick.en: to become attached to something, often with glue

ex.en: The stamp didn't stick to the envelope.

stick.en: to continue doing something and not change it

ex.en: It's hard to stick to a diet during the holidays.

sticker.en: a small piece of paper or plastic with a picture or words that can be stuck onto something

ex.en: She put a sticker on her notebook.

sticky.en: covered with a substance that sticks to things

ex.en: My hands are sticky from the candy.
It was a sticky summer afternoon.

sticky.en: (of a situation) difficult or awkward to deal with

ex.en: He found himself in a sticky situation.

stir.en: to move a liquid or substance around with a spoon

ex.en: She stirred the soup slowly.

stir.en: to cause a strong feeling or reaction in someone

ex.en: The speech stirred the crowd.

stir.en: a sudden reaction of public excitement or interest

ex.en: The news caused quite a stir.

storage.en: the act or place of keeping things until they are needed

ex.en: We rent extra storage for our furniture.
ex.ko: 우리는 가구를 위해 추가 창고를 빌린다.

storage.en: the space on a computer or device used to keep data

ex.en: This phone has 256GB of storage.
ex.ko: 이 휴대폰은 256GB의 저장 용량을 가지고 있다.

stormy.en: having strong winds and heavy rain

ex.en: The stormy weather kept us indoors.
ex.ko: 폭풍우가 치는 날씨 때문에 우리는 집안에 있었다.

stormy.en: full of strong emotions, arguments, or anger

ex.en: They had a stormy relationship.

storyteller.en: a person who tells stories, especially as entertainment

ex.en: Her grandfather was a wonderful storyteller.

strain.en: to injure or put too much stress on a part of your body by using it too hard

ex.en: He strained his back lifting the box.

strain.en: worry or pressure caused by a difficult situation

ex.en: The long hours put a strain on her health.
ex.ko: 긴 근무 시간이 그녀의 건강에 부담을 주었다.

strain.en: to make a great effort to do something

ex.en: She strained to hear the whispered conversation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 속삭이는 대화를 들으려고 안간힘을 썼다.

strangely.en: in a strange or unusual way

ex.en: He was acting strangely all day.

strategic.en: carefully planned to achieve a particular goal, especially in business or war

ex.en: The company made a strategic decision to expand overseas.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 해외 진출이라는 전략적 결정을 내렸다.

straw.cue: 음료를 마실 때 빠는 얇은 관
straw.en: a thin tube used for drinking a liquid

ex.en: She drank her juice through a straw.

straw.en: the dried stalks of wheat or other grain crops

ex.en: The farmer covered the ground with straw.

strawberry.en: a small, sweet red fruit with tiny seeds on its surface

ex.en: We picked fresh strawberries at the farm.
They crossed the stream on foot.

stream.en: to flow or move continuously in one direction

ex.en: Tears streamed down his face.

stream.en: to play video or audio directly from the internet without downloading it

ex.en: We streamed the movie on our tablet.

strengthen.en: to make something stronger, or to become stronger

ex.en: Regular exercise strengthens your muscles.

stress.en: to give special importance to something

ex.en: The teacher stressed the importance of practice.

stress.en: a feeling of worry and mental pressure caused by a difficult situation

ex.en: She's been under a lot of stress at work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장에서 스트레스를 많이 받고 있다.

stress.en: extra force put on a particular syllable or word when speaking

ex.en: The stress falls on the first syllable.

stressed.en: feeling worried and mentally tired because of pressure

ex.en: I've been really stressed about my exams.
ex.ko: 나는 시험 때문에 스트레스를 많이 받고 있다.

stressful.en: causing a lot of worry or mental pressure

ex.en: Moving to a new city can be stressful.
ex.ko: 새로운 도시로 이사하는 것은 스트레스가 클 수 있다.

stretch.en: to extend your arms, legs, or body to their full length

ex.en: She stretched before her morning run.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아침 조깅 전에 스트레칭을 했다.

stretch.en: to become longer or wider without breaking, or to pull something to make it longer

ex.en: This fabric stretches easily.

stretch.en: a continuous area or period of time

ex.en: He worked for a ten-hour stretch without a break.

stricken.en: badly affected by something such as illness, disaster, or grief

ex.en: The village was stricken by drought.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 가뭄으로 큰 피해를 입었다.

strip.en: to remove your clothes or someone else's clothes

ex.en: He stripped off his wet clothes.

strip.en: to take away someone's rights, power, or title

ex.en: He was stripped of his title after the scandal.

strip.en: a long, narrow piece of something

ex.en: Cut the paper into thin strips.

stripe.en: a long narrow band of color, different from the background

ex.en: The shirt has blue and white stripes.
ex.ko: 그 셔츠는 파란색과 흰색 줄무늬가 있다.

struggle.en: to try very hard to do something difficult

ex.en: She struggled to finish the marathon.

struggle.en: a long, difficult effort or fight

ex.en: It was a long struggle for independence.

stubborn.en: unwilling to change your mind or do what other people want

ex.en: My grandfather is too stubborn to see a doctor.
ex.ko: 우리 할아버지는 고집이 세서 병원에 가려 하지 않는다.

stuck.en: unable to move or escape from a place or position

ex.en: The car got stuck in the mud.
ex.ko: 차가 진흙에 빠져 꼼짝 못하게 되었다.

stuck.en: unable to make progress or find a solution to a problem

ex.en: I'm stuck on this math question.

studio.en: a room where an artist, photographer, or musician works

ex.en: The painter works in a small studio.

studio.en: a place where movies, TV shows, or music are recorded

ex.en: The show is filmed in a TV studio.

studio.en: a small apartment with one main room

ex.en: She rents a studio apartment downtown.
She gave the child a stuffed animal.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아이에게 속을 채운 동물 인형을 주었다.

stuffed.cue: 음식을 너무 많이 먹어 배가 꽉 찬
stuffed.en: having eaten so much food that you feel completely full

ex.en: I'm stuffed after that huge dinner.
ex.ko: 그 엄청난 저녁을 먹고 나니 배가 꽉 찼다.

stumble.en: to almost fall while walking because your foot hits something

ex.en: She stumbled over a rock on the trail.
ex.ko: 그녀는 산길에서 돌에 발이 걸려 비틀거렸다.

stumble.en: to find or meet something by chance

ex.en: I stumbled upon an old photo of my parents.
ex.ko: 나는 우연히 부모님의 옛날 사진을 발견했다.

stun.en: to shock or surprise someone so much that they cannot react

ex.en: The news stunned everyone in the room.
ex.ko: 그 소식은 방 안의 모두에게 충격을 주었다.

stun.en: to make someone unconscious or dizzy with a blow

ex.en: The boxer was stunned by a punch to the head.
ex.ko: 그 복서는 머리에 펀치를 맞고 정신을 잃을 뻔했다.

ex.en: She wore a stunning red dress.
ex.ko: 그녀는 굉장히 아름다운 빨간 드레스를 입었다.

ex.en: It was a stunning defeat for the champion.

stupid.en: not intelligent or sensible; showing a lack of thought

ex.en: It was stupid of me to forget my keys.
ex.ko: 내가 열쇠를 깜빡한 건 멍청한 짓이었다.

ex.en: She always wears stylish clothes.

subconscious.cue: 의식하지 못한 채 마음 깊은 곳에서
subconscious.en: existing in your mind without you being aware of it

ex.en: Her fear of water may be a subconscious memory of an accident.
ex.ko: 그녀의 물에 대한 두려움은 잠재의식 속 사고의 기억일 수 있다.

subconscious.en: the part of your mind that affects your behavior without you realizing it

ex.en: Dreams reveal a lot about our subconscious.
ex.ko: 꿈은 우리의 잠재의식에 대해 많은 것을 알려준다.

submarine.en: a ship that can travel underwater, especially used by the navy

ex.en: The submarine dove deep into the ocean.

substantial.en: large in amount, size, or importance

ex.en: She received a substantial pay raise.

substantial.en: based on fact and real, not just an idea

ex.en: There is substantial evidence to support the theory.
ex.ko: 그 이론을 뒷받침할 실질적인 증거가 있다.

substitute.en: to use or put something/someone in place of another

ex.en: You can substitute butter for oil in this recipe.
ex.ko: 이 레시피에서는 버터를 기름 대신 쓸 수 있다.

substitute.en: a person or thing that takes the place of another

ex.en: The teacher was absent, so a substitute took the class.
ex.ko: 선생님이 결석해서 대체 교사가 수업을 맡았다.

ex.en: Suddenly, the lights went out.

suffer.en: to experience pain, illness, or something unpleasant

ex.en: He suffers from severe headaches.

suffer.en: to be badly affected by something such as damage or loss

ex.en: The company suffered huge losses last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 큰 손실을 입었다.

ex.en: We don't have sufficient time to finish this today.
ex.ko: 우리는 오늘 이 일을 끝낼 충분한 시간이 없다.

suffix.en: a group of letters added to the end of a word to change its meaning

ex.en: Adding '-ness' as a suffix turns 'kind' into 'kindness'.
The organization offers help to prevent suicide.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 자살을 예방하기 위해 도움을 제공한다.

summarize.en: to give a short statement of the main points of something

ex.en: Please summarize the article in three sentences.
ex.ko: 그 기사를 세 문장으로 요약해 주세요.

ex.en: They finally reached the summit after six hours.
ex.ko: 그들은 여섯 시간 만에 마침내 정상에 도달했다.

summit.en: an important meeting between leaders of different countries

ex.en: The two presidents met at a climate summit.

sunbathe.en: to sit or lie in the sun to make your skin tan

ex.en: They sunbathed on the beach all afternoon.
ex.ko: 그들은 오후 내내 해변에서 일광욕을 했다.

sundial.en: a device that shows the time using the shadow of the sun

ex.en: The old garden has a stone sundial.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 정원에는 돌로 만든 해시계가 있다.

sunrise.en: the time when the sun rises in the morning

ex.en: We woke up early to watch the sunrise.

sunset.en: the time in the evening when the sun goes down

ex.en: The sky turned orange at sunset.

superior.en: better than others in quality or ability

ex.en: This laptop has superior battery life.

superior.en: a person of higher rank or position than you

ex.en: She reported the issue to her superior.

superstition.en: a belief that is not based on reason or fact, often about luck

ex.en: It's a superstition that breaking a mirror brings bad luck.
ex.ko: 거울을 깨면 불운이 온다는 것은 미신이다.

supply.en: to give or provide something that is needed

ex.en: The farm supplies eggs to local stores.
ex.ko: 그 농장은 지역 상점에 달걀을 공급한다.

supply.en: an amount of something that is available to be used

ex.en: The city's water supply is running low.
ex.ko: 그 도시의 물 공급이 부족해지고 있다.

supporter.en: a person who supports a team, person, or idea

ex.en: Thousands of supporters cheered for the team.
ex.ko: 수천 명의 응원단이 그 팀을 응원했다.

supportive.en: giving help, encouragement, or approval to someone

ex.en: My family has always been supportive of my choices.
ex.ko: 우리 가족은 항상 내 선택을 지지해 주었다.

suppose.en: to think that something is likely to be true

ex.en: I suppose she's still at work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아직 직장에 있을 것 같다.

suppose.en: to imagine that something is true, for the sake of discussion

ex.en: Suppose you won the lottery — what would you do?
ex.ko: 만약 네가 복권에 당첨된다면 무엇을 할 거야?

supposedly.en: according to what people say, although it may not be true

ex.en: The restaurant is supposedly the best in town.
ex.ko: 그 식당이 이 동네에서 최고라고들 한다.

surely.en: used to say that you are confident something is true

ex.en: Surely you remember her name?

surface.en: the outside or top layer of something

ex.en: The lake's surface was completely still.

surface.en: to become known or noticeable after being hidden

ex.en: New evidence surfaced during the trial.
The surgeon operated on her knee.

surgery.en: a medical operation to treat an illness or injury

ex.en: He needs surgery to fix his knee.

surprisingly.en: in a way that is unexpected or surprising

ex.en: Surprisingly, the test was quite easy.

surround.en: to be or go all around something on every side

ex.en: Mountains surround the small village.
ex.ko: 산들이 그 작은 마을을 둘러싸고 있다.

surrounding.en: existing around or near a place

ex.en: Tourists visit the castle and the surrounding villages.
ex.ko: 관광객들은 그 성과 인근 마을들을 방문한다.

survival.en: the state of continuing to live, especially after a difficult or dangerous situation

ex.en: Clean water is essential for survival.

survivor.en: a person who continues to live after an accident, disaster, or illness

ex.en: The rescue team found three survivors.

suspect.en: to believe that something is probably true, without being sure

ex.en: I suspect she already knows the answer.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 이미 답을 알고 있을 거라고 짐작한다.

suspect.en: to believe that someone may be guilty of something wrong

ex.en: Police suspect him of the robbery.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그가 강도 사건에 연루되었다고 의심한다.

suspect.en: a person who the police think may have committed a crime

ex.en: The suspect was arrested at the airport.

suspicion.en: a feeling that something bad or dishonest may be true

ex.en: The police had suspicions about his story.

swarm.en: a large group of insects moving together

ex.en: A swarm of bees flew past us.

swarm.en: (of people or animals) to move somewhere in a large group

ex.en: Tourists swarmed the beach in summer.

swear.en: to make a serious promise to do something

ex.en: I swear I'll be there on time.

swear.en: to use rude or offensive language

ex.en: He swore loudly when he stubbed his toe.
ex.ko: 그는 발가락을 찧고 크게 욕을 했다.

sweatshirt.en: a warm, loose piece of clothing with long sleeves, usually made of thick cotton

ex.en: He wore a gray sweatshirt to the gym.
ex.ko: 그는 체육관에 회색 맨투맨을 입고 갔다.

swell.en: to become larger or rounder than normal

ex.en: Her ankle swelled after the fall.

swell.en: to increase gradually in size or number

ex.en: The crowd swelled to thousands.

swimmer.en: a person who swims, especially regularly or well

ex.en: She is a strong swimmer.

switch.en: a small device used to turn something on or off

ex.en: Flip the switch to turn on the light.

switch.en: to change from one thing to another

ex.en: She switched jobs last month.

swollen.en: larger than normal because of swelling

ex.en: His eye was swollen after the accident.

sword.en: a weapon with a long sharp blade and a handle

ex.en: The knight carried a sword at his side.
ex.ko: 그 기사는 옆구리에 검을 차고 있었다.

sympathy.en: a feeling of caring about someone else's sadness or trouble

ex.en: She sent her sympathy to the grieving family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 슬픔에 잠긴 가족에게 위로를 전했다.

symptom.cue: 병이 있다는 것을 보여주는 신체 신호
symptom.en: a sign in your body that shows you have an illness

ex.en: A high fever is a common symptom of flu.

syndrome.cue: 특정 병을 나타내는 여러 증상의 조합
syndrome.en: a group of symptoms that together indicate a particular medical condition

ex.en: Doctors diagnosed him with chronic fatigue syndrome.

systematic.en: done according to a fixed plan or method, in an organized way

ex.en: She took a systematic approach to solving the problem.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문제 해결에 체계적인 접근법을 취했다.

tablet.cue: 손으로 쓸 수 있는 얇은 컴퓨터
tablet.en: a small, flat computer that you use by touching the screen

ex.en: She reads books on her tablet.

tablet.en: a small, hard, round piece of medicine that you swallow

ex.en: Take one tablet twice a day.
ex.ko: 하루에 두 번 알약 하나씩 드세요.

takeaway.en: food bought at a restaurant to eat somewhere else

ex.en: We ordered a takeaway on Friday night.
ex.ko: 우리는 금요일 밤에 포장 음식을 주문했다.

takeaway.en: the main point or lesson you get from something

ex.en: The key takeaway from the meeting was to cut costs.
ex.ko: 그 회의의 핵심은 비용을 줄이자는 것이었다.

takeoff.en: the moment when an aircraft leaves the ground

ex.en: Please fasten your seatbelt for takeoff.

tale.en: a story, especially an old or imaginative one

ex.en: She told the children a fairy tale.

talented.en: having a natural ability to do something well

ex.en: He is a talented young musician.
My little sister is very talkative.

tank.en: a large container for holding liquid or gas

ex.en: Fill the car's gas tank.

tank.en: a heavy armored military vehicle with guns

ex.en: The army sent tanks to the border.

taste.en: the flavor of food or drink that you notice in your mouth

ex.en: I love the taste of fresh mango.

taste.en: to notice or test the flavor of food, or to have a particular flavor

ex.en: This soup tastes a bit salty.

taste.en: a personal liking for a particular style or type of thing

ex.en: Her taste in music is very different from mine.
ex.ko: 그녀의 음악 취향은 나와 매우 다르다.

ex.en: The soup was watery and tasteless.

tasteless.en: showing bad judgment about what is socially or morally acceptable

ex.en: His joke about the accident was tasteless.
ex.ko: 그 사고에 대한 그의 농담은 몰상식했다.

ex.en: This curry is really tasty.

tax.en: money that people or businesses must pay to the government

ex.en: We pay income tax every year.

teaching.en: the job or activity of helping students learn

ex.en: She has been in teaching for twenty years.

teaching.en: an idea or belief that is taught, especially by a religion

ex.en: The teachings of Buddha focus on compassion.

teammate.en: a person who is on the same team as you

ex.en: He passed the ball to his teammate.

teamwork.en: the combined effort of a group working well together

ex.en: The project succeeded because of great teamwork.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 뛰어난 팀워크 덕분에 성공했다.

tease.en: to make fun of someone playfully

ex.en: His friends teased him about his new haircut.

technique.en: a particular way of doing something skillfully

ex.en: She learned a new painting technique.
We are living in an age of rapid technological change.
ex.ko: 우리는 급격한 기술 변화의 시대에 살고 있다.

teen.en: a young person between the ages of 13 and 19

ex.en: Many teens spend hours on social media.
ex.ko: 많은 십대들이 소셜미디어에 여러 시간을 보낸다.

telecommunications.cue: 전화·인터넷 등 먼 거리 통신 기술
telecommunications.en: the technology of sending information over long distances, such as by phone or internet

ex.en: He works in the telecommunications industry.

telegram.en: a short written message sent quickly using electrical signals, used before phones and email were common

ex.en: She received a telegram announcing his arrival.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 도착을 알리는 전보를 받았다.

teller.en: a bank employee who handles customers' money

ex.en: The teller counted out the cash carefully.
ex.ko: 그 은행 창구 직원은 현금을 조심스럽게 세었다.

temper.en: a tendency to become angry quickly, or a sudden feeling of anger

ex.en: He lost his temper and shouted at his brother.
ex.ko: 그는 화를 참지 못하고 동생에게 소리쳤다.

temporary.en: lasting for only a limited time, not permanent

ex.en: She found a temporary job for the summer.

tempt.en: to make someone want to do or have something, especially something not good for them

ex.en: The smell of cookies tempted her to eat one.
ex.ko: 쿠키 냄새가 그녀를 유혹해서 하나 먹게 만들었다.

tend.en: to usually do or be something

ex.en: She tends to arrive early for meetings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의에 일찍 도착하는 경향이 있다.

tend.en: to take care of someone or something

ex.en: Nurses tended the wounded soldiers.

tendency.en: a way of behaving that a person or thing usually has

ex.en: He has a tendency to be late.

tense.en: nervous and unable to relax because you are worried

ex.en: She felt tense before the interview.

tense.en: the form of a verb that shows when an action happens

ex.en: The past tense of 'go' is 'went'.

tension.en: a feeling of unfriendliness or worry between people

ex.en: There was tension between the two countries.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라 사이에 긴장이 있었다.

tension.en: the state of being stretched tight

ex.en: Adjust the tension of the guitar string.

tent.en: a shelter made of cloth, used for camping

ex.en: We pitched our tent near the lake.

term.en: a word or phrase used to describe a particular thing

ex.en: 'Photosynthesis' is a scientific term.

term.en: one of the periods that a school year is divided into

ex.en: The fall term starts in September.

term.en: (usually plural) the conditions of an agreement or contract

ex.en: Please read the terms of the contract carefully.

terminal.en: a building where passengers get on or off planes, buses, or trains

ex.en: Our flight departs from Terminal 2.

terminal.cue: 치료할 수 없어 죽음에 이르는 병
terminal.en: (of an illness) leading to death and unable to be cured

ex.en: He was diagnosed with terminal cancer.
I'm terribly sorry for the mistake.
We had a terrific time at the party.
ex.ko: 우리는 파티에서 정말 굉장한 시간을 보냈다.

ex.en: She was terrified of the dark as a child.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어릴 때 어둠을 몹시 무서워했다.

terror.en: an overwhelming feeling of extreme fear

ex.en: He screamed in terror when he saw the snake.
ex.ko: 그는 뱀을 보고 공포에 질려 비명을 질렀다.

terrorism.en: the use of violence for political purposes, especially against ordinary people

ex.en: The government increased security after the terrorism threat.
ex.ko: 정부는 테러 위협 이후 보안을 강화했다.

ex.en: I'm thankful for my family's support.
The store reported a theft of electronics.

theory.en: an idea or set of ideas that explains facts or events

ex.en: Darwin's theory of evolution changed science.

theory.cue: 확실친 않지만 그럴 것이라는 개인적 추측
theory.en: a personal guess or opinion about why something happened

ex.en: My theory is that he forgot the meeting.
ex.ko: 내 추측으로는 그가 회의를 잊어버린 것 같다.

thirst.en: the physical need or desire to drink

ex.en: After the run, he felt intense thirst.
ex.ko: 달리기 후 그는 극심한 갈증을 느꼈다.

thirst.en: a strong desire for something, not related to drinking

ex.en: She has a thirst for knowledge.

thorough.en: done carefully and completely, missing no details

ex.en: The police conducted a thorough investigation.

threat.en: a statement or possibility of something dangerous or harmful happening

ex.en: Climate change is a serious threat to the planet.

threatening.cue: 위험하거나 나쁜 일이 일어날 것 같은
threatening.en: making you feel that something dangerous or bad may happen

ex.en: He used threatening language during the argument.
ex.ko: 그는 말다툼 중에 위협적인 말을 썼다.

thriller.en: a book, movie, or play with an exciting, suspenseful story

ex.en: We watched a great thriller last night.

throughout.en: during the whole of a period of time

ex.en: It rained throughout the night.

throughout.en: in every part of a place

ex.en: The news spread throughout the country.

thumb.en: the short thick finger on the side of your hand

ex.en: He hurt his thumb while chopping vegetables.

thump.cue: 손이나 물건으로 세게 쳐서 쿵 소리
thump.en: to hit something hard, making a loud dull sound

ex.en: He thumped the table in anger.
ex.ko: 그는 화가 나서 탁자를 쾅 쳤다.

ex.en: She could hear the thump of her own heartbeat.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 심장이 쿵쿵 뛰는 소리를 들을 수 있었다.

thunder.cue: 번개 뒤에 들리는 크고 우르릉거리는 소리
thunder.en: the loud noise that comes from the sky during a storm, after lightning

ex.en: The thunder scared the little dog.

thunderous.cue: 귀청이 떨어질 정도로 아주 큰 소리

ex.en: The crowd gave a thunderous applause.

thus.en: as a result of what has just been said; therefore

ex.en: She studied hard, thus passing the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열심히 공부했고, 따라서 시험에 합격했다.

tick.en: (of a clock) to make a regular clicking sound

ex.en: The old clock ticked loudly on the wall.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 시계는 벽에서 큰 소리로 째깍거렸다.

tick.en: a small mark (✓) used to show that something is correct or has been done

ex.en: Put a tick next to the items you've finished.
ex.ko: 완료한 항목 옆에 체크 표시를 하세요.

tide.en: the regular rise and fall of the sea's level

ex.en: The tide comes in twice a day.

tighten.en: to make something tighter or firmer

ex.en: He tightened the screws on the shelf.
She held the baby tightly.

tile.en: a flat, thin piece of material used to cover floors, walls, or roofs

ex.en: The bathroom floor is covered with white tiles.
ex.ko: 욕실 바닥은 흰색 타일로 덮여 있다.

timely.en: happening at exactly the right time

ex.en: Her timely advice saved the project.

tin.en: a soft, silvery metal that does not rust easily

ex.en: The toy soldiers were made of tin.

tin.en: (British) a metal container used for food, such as a can

ex.en: She opened a tin of beans.
The kitten was tiny enough to fit in one hand.
ex.ko: 그 새끼 고양이는 한 손에 들어갈 만큼 아주 작았다.

tire.en: to become or make someone feel weak or in need of rest

ex.en: Long meetings tire me out.

tire.en: to become bored or lose interest in something

ex.en: He never tires of telling that story.
ex.ko: 그는 그 이야기를 하는 데 결코 싫증 내지 않는다.

ex.en: It was a long and tiring journey.

tissue.cue: 코를 풀거나 닦을 때 쓰는 얇은 종이
tissue.en: a soft, thin paper used for wiping or blowing your nose

ex.en: She grabbed a tissue to wipe her tears.

tissue.en: a group of cells in a plant or animal body that work together

ex.en: Muscle tissue helps the body move.

tobacco.en: the dried leaves of a plant, used for making cigarettes

ex.en: He grows tobacco on his farm.

tongue.en: the soft movable part inside your mouth used for tasting and speaking

ex.en: He burned his tongue on the hot coffee.

tongue.en: a language, especially someone's native language

ex.en: English is not her native tongue.

toothpaste.en: a substance used with a toothbrush to clean your teeth

ex.en: Squeeze some toothpaste onto your brush.

tornado.en: a violent, spinning storm shaped like a funnel that can destroy buildings

ex.en: A tornado destroyed several houses in the town.
ex.ko: 토네이도가 그 마을의 여러 집을 파괴했다.

toss.en: to throw something lightly or carelessly

ex.en: She tossed the ball to her friend.
He tossed the empty can into the trash.
The total cost was $200.

total.en: the whole amount when everything is added together

ex.en: The total came to fifty dollars.

total.en: to add up to a particular amount

ex.en: The bill totaled $85.
